title: Act I — Hallway to Study Bear

references.@Image1: character sheet — subject appearance, wardrobe, hair, makeup
references.@Image2: style plate — American manor lighting, champagne grade, 50mm lens feel
references.@Image3: Chunk 1 storyboard grid — shot composition and sequence

prompt:
@Image1's character as the subject, gold floral wrap dress, voluminous blonde hair, heavy gold earrings. She speaks throughout with a posh British accent — flat monotone, matter-of-fact grocery-list cadence. Scene and color grade reference @Image2. Shot sequence reference @Image3. Warm prestige American manor, cream walls honey hardwood oil portraits brass lamps soft tungsten light, 50mm shallow depth of field warm champagne grade, deadpan blocking sincere camera, 14 seconds.

0–2s: Medium wide locked-off manor hallway, butler in black tuxedo white gloves presents silver platter with raw steak, she picks up steak looks once tosses casually off-screen left like discarding napkin, butler does not react, quiet hall ambience.

2–4s: Hard cut close-up 50mm static, face fills frame, looks directly into lens jaw relaxed matter-of-fact grocery-list cadence, posh British monotone dialogue begins.

4–7s: Wide stabilized tracking gallery hallway, she strides away from camera mid-anecdote oil paintings both sides, camera tracks ahead of her retreating, she does not look at camera, footsteps on honey hardwood.

7–10s: Wide slow dolly-in warm traditional study, pink velvet wingback beside brass floor lamp Persian rug dark wood bookshelves, she crosses sits crosses one leg settles back looks at camera completely still.

10–14s: Hard cut wide locked-off from across study, she seated pink velvet wingback, large well-groomed calm brown bear upright in identical wingback immediate right paws in lap, she does not look at bear continues speaking to camera, static hold warm lamplight neither acknowledges the other.

Voice: posh British female, flat monotone, very fast clipped pace, no pauses.

Dialogue: I don't eat red meat before four o'clock. My neighbour has this gnome in her garden that she dresses for the seasons. Last winter it wore a tiny parka, which felt sincere. In July it had a swimsuit on. That felt like a cry for help. Anyway, she asked if I wanted to borrow it for a party and I said absolutely not, I don't borrow statuary, I don't borrow spandex, I don't borrow regret, she's still texting me about hydration, I've muted her, I've muted Tuesdays, Tuesdays are aggressively middle.
